title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is practical: more work is moving from abstract principles to concrete evaluation, assurance, and deployment controls. The most relevant updates focus on secure AI evaluations, external red teaming of internal monitoring systems, and emerging governance frameworks for assurance and manipulation risks.

items.title: NIST CAISI signs OpenMined agreement for secure AI evaluations
items.why_it_matters: Secure evaluation infrastructure could enable more sensitive model testing without exposing models, data, or evaluators.

items.title: METR details red-teaming of Anthropic’s internal agent monitoring systems
items.why_it_matters: External testing of internal monitoring probes how well labs can detect and contain risky agent behavior.

items.title: Partnership on AI examines assurance as a path to trustworthy AI
items.why_it_matters: Assurance frameworks could shape audits, evidence standards, and accountability for high-risk AI deployments.

items.title: Google DeepMind outlines safeguards against harmful manipulation
items.why_it_matters: Manipulation-focused safety work targets a core misuse and model-behavior risk in sensitive domains.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, and policy organizations, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
